Figure 11 Parameter Analysis of MGSM on Three Datasets. ADP refers to adaptive ξ adjustment.

Table 7 Comparison of Average Forgetting. We report Favg (in %) for representative incremental methods; lower is better. Bold and underline mark the lowest and second-lowest forgetting under each protocol.

Figure 1 Overview of the prior RPL-based CIL paradigm and comparison of initial-stage RPL construction. (a) Prior Methods: After first stage adaptation, frozen PTM extracts features Zinit, which are projected through a randomly initialized RPL (WRPL) to obtain highdimensional features Hinit, followed by computing classifier weights Wβ . During incremental learning, new features Zt pass through the same frozen WRPL to Ht, and only Wβ is updated to W (t) β via recursive ridge regression. (b) Our Method: We leverage the initial task and PTM to inform MGSM-guided RPL construction. During incremental learning, Wβ is updated recursively as in (a).
Figure 16 Eigenvalue and condition number analysis of Pt. Left: maximum eigenvalue. Center: minimum eigenvalue. Right: condition number of Pt.
Figure 10 Comparison of Training Time Across Representative CIL Methods.

Table 1 Comparison of CIL Performance Across Methods and Benchmarks. Bold and underline mark the best and second-best results. Alast and Aavg denote final and average accuracy (%). B-m Inc-n (m: initial classes, n: incremental classes; m=0: equal split). Joint linear probe trains only the classifier head on all tasks, while Joint fine-tuning updates the entire PTM on all tasks.

Figure 14 Impact of first-session adaptation on representative RPL-based methods. We report ∆Alast relative to the corresponding Joint Linear Probe baseline on four datasets. Dark bars indicate variants with FSA, and light bars indicate variants without FSA. Larger positive values mean that the incremental learner exceeds the frozen-backbone joint linear probe by a wider margin.
Figure 13 Illustration of the MGSM-driven RPL construction process.
Figure 15 Top-1 accuracy during sequential continual learning using ViT-B/16-IN21K.

Figure 2 Overview of MGSM-guided RPL construction in SCL-MGSM. Data from any stage can serve as the initialization set to build the RPL from scratch. Random hidden units are progressively sampled, evaluated by MGSM, and appended to the RPL only if they satisfy the supervisory criterion. The construction terminates once the residual converges below a predefined threshold ε. See Appendix B for details.
